title: Video Games The Ideal Evangelion Game
display_title: Video Games The Ideal Evangelion Game
wikitext:
[]: [Video Games] The Ideal Evangelion Game             Quote Post by Squigsquasher » Tue Jan 28, 2014 8:13 am So, there's been a distinct lack of good Evangelion games. To the best of my knowledge, the PSP/PS2 games were great, and the N64 game was reasonably OK, but most other games were a bit iffy, such as GFOS (which is closer to my canon-raping CYOA than the actual series) and some other obscure Saturn games. So, if you were in charge of making an Eva game, what would it be like? Post any details you think are relevant (platform, genre etc) as well as a basic description. If I were doing one, I'd do it like this: -Multiplatform, if an exclusive then either WiiU for the touchpad capabilities or PC for keyboard and moddability. -3rd person. -Real time. -In each battle, you control a single Eva, with other Evas on AI. The controls are basic 3rd-person, but you can issue orders to the other Evas (such as ordering them to retreat and recharge, focus firepower on an Angel, attempt to erode an Angel's AT Field whilst you shoot it down, etc), which is where I think the WiiU's touchpad would come in handy. -In most missions, your Eva has the usual 5 minutes of battery power. If you run out, you go to emergancy power, losing offensive and movement capabilities but retaining order issuing commands, allowing another Eva to connect you to a cable. If all Evas are knocked out/run out of charge, the mission is failed. -Combat varies depending on mission, but in most missions it uses standard TPS shooting controls and a fairly basic close combat system (not entirely sure how you'd do this what with the whole AT field erosion system). -Maybe possibility of going Berserk/Beast Mode, woud need extra thought. -Follows its own storyline independent of the series and Rebuild. Maybe have extra missions with special enemies, such as the Diemay Angel or a rogue T-Riden-T Land Cruiser. -Multiplayer mode pits a team of Evas against a team of Angels, with different game modes. Angels are more powerful than Evas, but there must always be more Evas than Angels in a game (if there's less, say in a 1-1 game, then mechanics can balance out power levels). Players can create custom Evas/Angels and pilots to play with in multiplayer only. Anyway, that's my idea. What about you?

[]:             Quote Post by Melchior » Tue Jan 28, 2014 8:55 am I'd like a game like "Typing of the Dead", but it will be from Ritsuko's point of view. Anyone unfamiliar with the game: SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Tue Jan 28, 2014 1:58 pm Angel Fighting component by NetherRealm Between Angel fights an RPG/Dating Sim by Atlus in the vein of the Persona series. CGI Cutscenes by Blizzard Designed exclusively for Oculus Rift.

[]:             Quote Post by A.T. Fish » Tue Jan 28, 2014 2:18 pm 1st person shooter (kind of) where you control Kensuke, armed with a camera your mission is to collect a photographic record of every Angel battle. Battles happen in the background independent of your control and all you can do is try to find a good spot to take the perfect shots. The platform must support augmented reality.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Tue Jan 28, 2014 3:00 pm Dating sim where all the evas and angels are available to court.

[]:             Quote Post by SimplyMason0 » Tue Jan 28, 2014 3:34 pm A TellTale like game which is mostly story based but still having combat in form of puzzles. If you look at Eva, its not that action based by the pilots themselves. Even the 64 version had to use berserk modes for stages. Eva main story is drama than the actual action. However I don't think Shinji or the other characters seem that video gamy. Fans wouldn't do the things Shinji and Asuka did out of choice in a video game. So I think Kaworu would be appropriate since he is more out going and the game could be about opening Shinji, Asuka and Rei up.

[]:             Quote Post by Giji Shinka » Thu Jan 30, 2014 5:53 am Combine Asura's wrath and Evangelion =WIN

[]:             Quote Post by Gorbatschow » Thu Jan 30, 2014 9:59 am Giji Shinka wrote: Combine Asura's wrath and Evangelion =WIN

[]:             Quote Post by Giji Shinka » Thu Jan 30, 2014 12:18 pm ^Asuka's wrath...? SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Gorbatschow » Thu Jan 30, 2014 12:30 pm Giji Shinka wrote: ^Asuka's wrath...? SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Fri Jan 31, 2014 12:48 am Strategy game involving all original, Rebuild, and video game Evas, pilots, mecha like Jet Alone and TRIDENT, vehicles, and Angels, all weighted out somehow that makes sense with maybe a campaign mode in there. Team battles allow for multiple Angels at once with a limit, opposing team also has a limit on mecha/total forces deployed depending on who is fighting who. As Nerv you control Eva units including Eva-03, -04, -05, Mark 6, ground installations, and UN forces with options to bring in extracanonical mechs. Access to all types of Eva weaponry and conventional and N2 ordnance. All except Mark 6 rely on umbilical cables which can be severed. When the battery runs out, the unit is KO. In Eva-01 vs. Zeruel battles, Eva-01 can obtain the S2 Engine but must fulfill certain actions. As Angels/Seele, you control Angels, MPEs, and JSSDF. Angels can regenerate HP but have limited use of AT Fields and extra defensive and offensive power. However if they take too much damage their core HP starts to become affected. As Wille you control Eva-02, Eva-08, Eva 8+2, Wunder and fleet. As Nerv 3.0 you control Mark 4s, Mark 9, Eva-13, and MPEs. Or Raising sim focusing on Rei and Asuka, each with a route, but the player controls them and is not a third party.

[]:             Quote Post by Bhorium » Mon Feb 03, 2014 10:12 am I think it would be most interesting if an Eva game would explore another part of the setting, and was set outside the series' timeline, but also dealt with some of the same themes as the series proper. In that regard, I have two ideas: An RPG with isometric viewpoint, set in the year 2046, which starts with the player character emerging from the LCL sea. For some time, the post-3rd impact societies in Japan and eastern China has been rebuilding in relative mutual peace, to due the feelings of a shared connection from the Instrumentally event, but with no central government, political tensions between the various settlements and towns are starting to brew as the first post-Impact generation is slowly starting to assume power and responsibility, and things are not made better as scattered reports of people with abilities that seem to go far beyond what humans normally are capable of, are spreading paranoia throughout the region. An point-and-click adventure game. The player character are a covert operative for an mysterious organisation, and together with a partner, a skilled technician, he has been tasked to infiltrate a large and abandoned military research-base in Massachusetts, USA and extract some information the organisation considers of the utmost importance. As the game progresses, you learn more about the player character, the partner, and the organisation they work for, through their conversations, while datalogs and diaries from the employees of the base helps filling you in on the backstory.

[]:             Quote Post by Clover » Mon Feb 03, 2014 7:21 pm Terrorists have taken control of a remote Angel disposal facility in Alaska, and are demanding the remains of Adam. If their demands are not met within 24 hours, they'll launch an N2 ICBM. Turns out the facility is actually being used as a construction and test site for a secret Evangelion unit capable of deploying anywhere in the world. EVANGELION

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Mon Feb 03, 2014 7:42 pm I would go for an RPG style game. Perhaps it could focus on some of the more human aspects of Eva, than just the robots. You could take your pick to play as one of the three main children.

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Mon Feb 03, 2014 8:44 pm Clover wrote: Terrorists have taken control of a remote Angel disposal facility in Alaska, and are demanding the remains of Adam. If their demands are not met within 24 hours, they'll launch an N2 ICBM. Turns out the facility is actually being used as a construction and test site for a secret Evangelion unit capable of deploying anywhere in the world. EVANGELION

[]:             Quote Post by Clover » Tue Feb 04, 2014 10:35 am CJD wrote: After that intro I was expecting a Bad Dudes reference. I was sorely disappointed.

[]:             Quote Post by Ray » Tue Mar 11, 2014 2:57 pm Something like the Walking dead or Wolf Among Us. You play as Shinji Ikari, but unlike the original series where you have to watch how Shinji reacts you can control how he reacts. His reactions influence the world around him, and could very well change the direction of the story and the inevitable ending.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Tue Mar 11, 2014 9:39 pm I'm going to mention the inevitable ultimate dating sim before anyone else does. But I want a Rei ending instead of just Asuka and Mana!

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Tue Mar 11, 2014 10:08 pm Don't worry Kendrix. When I win the $400,000,000 lottery I plan on using a substantial amount of it to create the ultimate Eva dating sim.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Tue Mar 11, 2014 11:11 pm Skyrim mod.
